title: Voter Registration
description: Any Indian citizen who is 18 or older on 1 January of the qualifying year can register. You fill Form 6 online at voterportal.eci.gov.in or the NVSP / Voter Helpline app, or submit it on paper to your Booth Level Officer (BLO). Once verified, your name is added to the electoral roll and you receive an EPIC (Electoral Photo Identity Card) — the voter ID issued by the Election Commission of India.
safeguards: Claims and objections period allows the public to flag errors or duplicates in the draft roll
BLOs conduct door-to-door verification in their assigned polling area
Aadhaar-EPIC linking (voluntary, under the Election Laws Amendment Act 2021) helps detect duplicate entries
Special Summary Revision published annually to update rolls

title: Election Announcement
description: The Election Commission of India announces the schedule — polling phases, nomination dates, and counting day. From the moment of announcement, the Model Code of Conduct (MCC) takes effect: governments cannot announce new schemes, transfer officials without ECI approval, or use state resources for campaigning. Candidates file nomination papers with an affidavit disclosing assets, liabilities, and any criminal cases.
safeguards: Model Code of Conduct restricts ruling parties from misusing office
Mandatory criminal-record affidavit (Form 26) for every candidate
Expenditure ceilings per candidate, monitored by ECI observers
Paid news and hate speech flagged by Media Certification & Monitoring Committees
source: ECI Handbook for Candidates · Representation of the People Act, 1951

title: Polling Day
tagline: Casting your vote on an EVM
description: On polling day, you visit your assigned polling station (check at electoralsearch.eci.gov.in). After the Polling Officer verifies your identity against the roll using your EPIC or one of 11 alternate documents, your finger is marked with indelible ink. You press the button next to your chosen candidate's name and symbol on the Electronic Voting Machine (EVM). The VVPAT (Voter Verifiable Paper Audit Trail) unit prints a slip showing your choice, which you can see through a glass window for 7 seconds before it drops into a sealed box.
safeguards: Indelible ink on the left index finger prevents double voting
EPIC or one of 11 ECI-approved alternate ID documents required
VVPAT paper slip lets you verify your vote was recorded correctly
Polling agents from each party observe proceedings inside the booth
Central Armed Police Forces deployed at sensitive booths
source: ECI · Manual on Electronic Voting Machine and VVPAT

title: Storage & Transport
description: After polling closes, EVMs are sealed in the presence of polling and candidate agents, with their signatures on the seals. They are transported under armed escort to a strongroom — a designated secured facility with 24×7 CCTV surveillance and multi-party observer access. Candidates' representatives can watch the strongroom from outside at any time until counting begins.
safeguards: Triple-layer sealing with candidate agents' signatures on all seals
Armed escort (CAPF) during transport to the strongroom
24×7 CCTV with feeds accessible to candidate representatives
Double-lock system: strongroom key held jointly by Returning Officer and observer
Randomisation of EVMs before and after polling to prevent pre-tampering
source: ECI · Compendium of Instructions on EVM and Strongroom Management

title: Counting & Result Declaration
description: On counting day, the strongroom is opened before all candidates' agents. Postal ballots are counted first, then EVM votes round by round at the Counting Centre. For every Assembly segment, VVPAT slips from 5 randomly selected polling stations are hand-counted and tallied against the EVM count — a Supreme Court–mandated verification. Once the Returning Officer is satisfied, results are declared and forwarded to the ECI for publication on results.eci.gov.in.
safeguards: Mandatory VVPAT verification of 5 random polling stations per Assembly segment
Candidates' agents present at every counting table
Round-by-round totals announced publicly before proceeding
Recounting provisions under Rule 63 of Conduct of Election Rules
Election petitions can be filed in the High Court within 45 days
source: Supreme Court judgment in N. Chandrababu Naidu v. Union of India (2019) · Conduct of Election Rules, 1961
